name: New York
localTips: Tap any contactless card or phone at subway turnstiles (OMNY) — fares cap at the weekly rate automatically, no MetroCard needed.
Walk like you mean it: don't stop mid-sidewalk, and let people off the train before boarding — the two unbreakable rules.
A dollar-fifty slice folded in half and eaten walking is a food group. The 99-cent places are for 3am only.
Many museums are 'pay-what-you-wish' for New York State residents, but the Met's full price applies to visitors — check before queueing.
Tipping is not optional: 20% at restaurants, a dollar or two per drink at bars.

activities.title: Brooklyn Bridge at sunrise
activities.description: Walk the wooden promenade above the traffic from Brooklyn toward Manhattan's skyline — sunrise beats the midday selfie crush by a mile.

activities.title: Central Park: Mall, Bethesda & the Ramble
activities.description: The elm cathedral of the Mall, Bethesda Terrace's tiled arcade and the wild paths of the Ramble — 15 minutes in, the city disappears.

activities.title: The High Line
activities.description: An elevated freight line turned wildflower walkway threading through Chelsea's architecture from Gansevoort to Hudson Yards.

activities.title: Staten Island Ferry past the Statue of Liberty
activities.description: The world's best free cruise — 25 minutes each way past Lady Liberty and the harbor, with the skyline receding behind you. Runs 24/7.

activities.title: Grand Central Terminal & whispering gallery
activities.description: The turquoise zodiac ceiling, the four-faced opal clock and the whispering arch outside the Oyster Bar — test it with a friend on the opposite corner.

activities.title: Greenwich Village & Washington Square
activities.description: Jazz doorways, brownstone lanes like Minetta and MacDougal, and the arch where buskers, chess hustlers and NYU life collide.

activities.title: DUMBO & Brooklyn Heights Promenade
activities.description: The Manhattan Bridge framed down Washington Street, then the Promenade's full-skyline panorama — Brooklyn's two greatest views, ten minutes apart.

activities.title: Chelsea Market
activities.description: A block-long former Nabisco factory of food stalls and shops under the High Line — lobster rolls, tacos and the city's best browsing when it pours.

activities.title: NY Public Library & Bryant Park
activities.description: The marble lions, the Rose Main Reading Room's cloud-painted ceiling, then coffee among Bryant Park's plane trees next door.

activities.title: Times Square after dark
activities.description: Yes it's chaos, but the LED canyon at night is a genuine spectacle — do it once, late, then escape to a quieter block for a nightcap.

activities.title: Roosevelt Island tram ride
activities.description: A subway-fare cable car floating beside the Queensboro Bridge — skyline views going over, riverside park and lighthouse walk on the island.

activities.title: Stone Street & Financial District history
activities.description: Cobblestones and 17th-century Dutch street plans hemmed in by skyscrapers — plus Federal Hall, the Charging Bull and Trinity Church in a tight loop.

activities.title: Chinatown dumpling crawl
activities.description: Fried pork dumplings at counter joints, egg tarts on Mott Street and the din of Canal — the cheapest great meal in Manhattan.

activities.title: Comedy Cellar drop-in sets
activities.description: The low-ceilinged Village basement where big names test new material unannounced — Chappelle, Rock and Schumer have all dropped in.
activities.insiderNote: Book the late show online days ahead; the Village Underground room next door has the same lineup and easier seats. Two-item minimum.

activities.title: Jazz at the Village Vanguard
activities.description: The wedge-shaped basement where Coltrane and Evans recorded live albums — 90 minutes of world-class jazz under the same red awning since 1935.
activities.insiderNote: Buy tickets online the Monday they release; the 10:30pm second set is easier to get and often looser.

activities.title: Proper bagel & appetizing shop breakfast
activities.description: Russ & Daughters' hand-sliced lox on a still-warm bagel — the 110-year-old Lower East Side 'appetizing' tradition at its peak.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the café's weekend queue: order at the original shop counter and eat in Seward Park two blocks south.

activities.title: Tenement Museum apartment tours
activities.description: Preserved immigrant apartments at 97 Orchard Street, told family by family — the most moving 90 minutes in New York.
activities.insiderNote: Each tour covers a different family and era — 'Hard Times' is the classic first visit. Book ahead; groups are tiny.

activities.title: Smorgasburg & Brooklyn Flea weekends
activities.description: A hundred food vendors on the Williamsburg waterfront Saturdays (Prospect Park Sundays) — ramen burgers to Filipino BBQ with skyline views.
activities.insiderNote: Go at opening (11am) — by 1pm the lines for the famous stalls hit 40 minutes. Cash speeds everything.

activities.title: Mmuseumm — a museum in a freight elevator
activities.description: The world's smallest museum: modern-artifact curiosities crammed into a Tribeca alley elevator shaft, viewable 24/7 through the window.
activities.insiderNote: It's on Cortlandt Alley — one of Manhattan's last true alleys and a film-location favorite. Two minutes, but you'll talk about it for days.

activities.title: Dollar-oyster happy hours
activities.description: From 4 to 7pm half the city's raw bars sell oysters for $1–2 apiece — briny luxury at dive prices, from the East Village to Grand Central's Oyster Bar.
activities.insiderNote: Weekdays only at most spots, and sit at the bar — shuckers comp regulars. Crown Shy's sister bars and Upstate are reliable picks.

activities.title: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt mirrored decks
activities.description: Three storeys of mirrored infinity rooms and glass ledges 1,000 feet above Grand Central — the city's most theatrical view. Book sunset early.
activities.bookingQuery: SUMMIT One Vanderbilt tickets New York

activities.title: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island
activities.description: Ferry to Liberty Island, then Ellis Island's registry hall where 12 million arrivals were processed — book pedestal or crown access weeks ahead.
activities.bookingQuery: Statue of Liberty Ellis Island ferry ticket New York

activities.title: The Met Fifth Avenue
activities.description: Two million works from Temple of Dendur to Rembrandt — pick three galleries and a rooftop drink (in season) rather than attempting it all.
activities.bookingQuery: Metropolitan Museum of Art admission ticket New York

activities.title: Broadway show
activities.description: The main event — book ahead for the big titles or try same-day rush and lottery apps for $30–50 seats to shows that usually run $200.

activities.title: Harbor lights evening cruise
activities.description: Circle lower Manhattan at dusk as the skyline switches on, sliding past the Statue of Liberty lit gold — the classic never misses.
activities.bookingQuery: New York harbor lights evening cruise

activities.title: 9/11 Memorial & Museum
activities.description: The twin reflecting pools and the museum beneath them — harrowing, essential and beautifully done. Allow more time than you think.
activities.bookingQuery: 9/11 Memorial Museum timed entry ticket New York

activities.title: Greenwich Village food & history tour
activities.description: Original Joe's slice, Faicco's rice balls and prohibition-era speakeasy history through the Village's prettiest blocks.
activities.bookingQuery: Greenwich Village food tour New York

activities.title: Manhattan helicopter tour
activities.description: Fifteen minutes over the Statue of Liberty, One World Trade and Central Park — the splurge that reorganizes your mental map of the city.
activities.bookingQuery: Manhattan helicopter sightseeing tour New York

activities.title: Ball game at Yankee Stadium
activities.description: Bleacher seats, a hot dog and the subway ride up with the crowd — baseball as New York's summer religion.
activities.bookingQuery: New York Yankees game tickets Yankee Stadium

activities.title: Bryant Park Winter Village & ice rink
activities.description: Free-admission ice rink (skate rental extra), holiday shops and hot cider stalls under the library's rear façade — Manhattan's coziest winter scene.
